video.title: João Almeida Takes the Leader's Jersey | Volta a Catalunya Stage 2 2021

transcript.clean_text: welcome back to the lantern rouge youtube channel into highlights of stage two of the volta cyclist catalonia 2021 the 18 and a half kilometer time trial doing a loop around banolis 200 meters plus elevation gain and quite a technical course lots of twists and turns a quick run into the finish with a few sharp corners and hasn't been a tt in catalonia for years and years and i think the reason they've inserted it is to ensure that there's gc gaps before the big mountain stages in three and four so that presumably people have lost time in the tt have to attack whereas the opposite to that is a rider like almeida who'd need to gain as much time as possible in this time trial ahead of those mountain stages where presumably he will lose some time to adam yates salman yates karapaz etc can someone let me know is this the famous era baselay or is it just a normal one the short favorite for the stage was remy cavania probably a bit too short in hindsight the french national champion in the time trial he lost a fair bit of time in the stage before so he wasn't in contention to take the leader's jersey just the stage when he'd had a good tt in paranese stage two on that short rolling course as well he was unlucky not to win that behind bisica 0.8 seconds behind when he got caught up in a corner in the last k or so so with cavania rolling out before rowan dennis we knew he'd be going into the hot seat the question was just how much but there were quick technical descent sections in this tt and i don't think that would have suited him as much compared to someone like rowan dennis who can really push it through those corners but nonetheless cavani still set a really solid time 35 seconds out of cherny with a time of 22-32 going into the hot seat but then it was rowan dennis who also wasn't in contention for the leaders jersey after the stage because he lost two minutes or so on stage one when inies had put him on the front to pull in the last five k's or so to pull back time into leonard kemner but we saw in yorkshire 2019 albeit on a much longer course but on a rolling technical course where you need to push it through some of the corners rowan dennis is still one of the best tt riders in the world and he was back in full force in this time trial he hadn't won a race yet for ineos since joining them at the start of the 2020 season i think his last win was the world championships in yorkshire that i just referred to in 2019 and this left-hand corner and the run into the finish is a good example of what i was talking about dennis pushed it to the max through all of the corners did a really really fast last fifteen hundred meters we don't unfortunately have regular intermediate time checks for this tt that we can rely upon to see where dennis gained the most time on remy cavania but he beat him by a full five seconds setting at best time of 22-27 that would not be beaten for the day then we had like an hour plus until the gc contenders started to roll out richie port the ineos grenadiers teammate of rowan dennis he crashed out of paris nice where he looked in pretty good condition there hasn't been much fanfare from him or ineos or the media for his return to racing in catalonia after that crash he had got stuck back into training pretty quickly after that parinis crash and he's a good time trollers and he's been one of the best one-week stage racers in the world in the last decade and just about on any other team he would be the undisputed gc leader at this catalonia and he showed in this tt that he still got it i mean we shouldn't be surprised planche to belfair he did a magic tt about six months ago as well but he set a time of 2301 just 35 seconds behind rowan dennis but he was actually beaten in this tt by stephen christwich he's very underrated christ like it's probably because he doesn't win a lot of races or do like very explosive or exciting things but he's at a time of 23 minutes 34 seconds behind rowan dennis which is going to be better than every other gc contender not named almeida or brandon mcnulty we saw in paranise stage 2tt he was only six seconds behind row and dennis there so chris fox put himself in a really good place with this tt result as well as brandon mcnulty the young american on uae team emirates we knew he'd do a good time but just how good would he beat almeida he set a time of 22 55 28 and a half seconds behind rowan dennis the best gkc contender up to that point until the young portuguese legend already almeida rolled out for de kernick quickstep he put big time into adam yates on a flat 13 kilometer tt in the uae tour i think 30 seconds plus it would be important for him in this tt to gain as much time as possible because in that uae tour he lost about 40 seconds plus to adam yates on jabel defeat the mountaintop finish on stage three didn't lose too much time at all though on the more gradual finish in stage five and then in toronto adriatic on the mountaintop finish to practitivo he lost about 30 to 35 seconds to simon yates so he'd be needing to put a buffer into those sort of riders in this tt ahead of the really nasty mountain top finish stages we have in stages three and four and he performed once again he's so consistent almeida and reliable in these time trials he also had the gc leaders jersey up for grabs if he was able to beat brandon mcnulty he was on the same time as him going into this stage obviously with dennis and cavania having lost too much time in stage one so almeida was the best of the gc contenders in this time trial catalonia setting a time of 22 54 28.2 seconds behind rowan dennis but the massive performance of the day and i don't have the images for you unfortunately is adam yates he lost that time in the uae to a stage two as i'd mentioned to almeida in over 13 ktt we had an 18k tt here and he only finished seven seconds behind almeida setting a time of 2302 just 35 seconds behind rowan dennis an absolutely monster tt from adam yates that puts him in the driver's seat for this year's catalonia rowan denis though took out the stage win five seconds out of cavanier no one else came even near to their times good to see dennis back on the top step as well as he's going to be an invaluable teammate for the ineos riders in the stages to come but here's the top 10 in the stage results dennis ahead of cavania almeida mcnulty christ like fifth port sixth that yates seven position is really good cherny eighth to bod ninth and grant thomas tenth 47 seconds behind rowan dennis almeida going into the leaders jersey a fraction of a second ahead of brandon mcnulty will he be able to hold that in the mountain stages to come it's going to be difficult for him here's the top 10 in the gc at the moment but that's not too helpful because there's other writers who aren't in gc contention in there so i put together a little list for you to show the gc standings of the writers i say other gc contenders i'll make the same time as mcnulty crossfire just five seconds behind mcnulty the problem for almeida and mcnulty is port and yates are at six and seven seconds on ineos and they're better climbers than almeida and mcnulty and we've got this beast of a stage today going from sea level at the canal olympic to catalonia up to volta 2125 meters altitude 203 kilometer stage the climb is false flat for the first 10ks gradually getting steeper until the climb proper is 12 k's at seven and a half percent we saw at the uae tour on jupiter feet i keep referring to it but similar finish and a lot of the gc contenders that we see here were there ku salmada and adam yates koos was aggressive on that stage he lost 55 seconds on the tt today not great not terrible 3.6 runkin but he was aggressive in the uae on her feet against adam yates and pagacha and it eventually cost him when they dropped him i'm not sure what jumbo business plan is going to be with christ like so far up on gc and coos 55 seconds behind but the writers who will need to be most aggressive on these mountain stages are hugh carthy simon yates and on rick martin losing 40 42 and 51 seconds respectively i think will actually be simon yates the brother of adam yates who will be looking to be most aggressive on these stages he's lost that much time but he's also got the climbing prowess to try and make it back up but i can't wait for those mountain stages in stages three and four if you want to see where it's broadcast live we've got it in a pin comment down below i hope you enjoyed the video like it down
